steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Before the blank: article + adverb (“The roughly”)

Blank: a noun phrase serving as the subject

After the blank: a verb and its complement (“is classified as an arid desert”)

The blank must contain a complete noun phrase with both the measurement and the proper noun name.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A subject noun phrase must not be separated from its verb by a comma. Additionally, when a measurement and a proper noun name form a single noun phrase, they should not be separated by commas—they work as one identifying unit. Option D correctly presents the noun phrase as a continuous unit with the verb immediately following and no internal commas. The other options either insert a comma between the subject and verb or disrupt the noun phrase with an internal comma separating the measurement from the name.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
